svg.title: Traditional Business Intelligence vs Big Data
svg.text: Traditional Business Intelligence vs Big Data

section.data-narration: Business Intelligence is a set of processes, technologies, and tools used to analyze and interpret data to support business decision-making.
section.text:
Focused on historical data
Uses data warehouses and reporting tools
Relies on structured data
Typically uses query languages like SQL

section.data-narration: Big Data refers to the vast amounts of structured, semi-structured, and unstructured data that organizations generate and collect from various sources. This data is characterized by its large size, complexity, and variety.
section.text:
Large volume
High velocity
High variety
Complex

section.data-narration: While Business Intelligence focuses on historical data to support business decision-making, Big Data encompasses a broader range of data from various sources, including social media, IoT devices, and customer interactions.
section.text: Data source: Traditional BI relies on structured data from databases, while Big Data comes from a wide range of unstructured data

section.data-narration: Traditional BI uses data warehouses and reporting tools to analyze and interpret data, while Big Data uses a wide range of tools and technologies to collect, process, and analyze data. Traditional BI focuses on supporting business decision-making, while Big Data is used to support business strategy and innovation.
section.text:
Use of data warehouses and reporting tools
Use of a wide range of tools and technologies
Focus on supporting business decision-making
Focus on supporting business strategy and innovation

section.data-narration: Big Data is used to support business strategy and innovation, while Traditional BI is used to support business decision-making. Big Data is used to analyze and interpret data from a wide range of sources, including social media, IoT devices, and customer interactions. Traditional BI is used to support business decision-making by analyzing and interpreting data from structured data sources, such as databases and data warehouses.
section.text:
Focus on business strategy and innovation
Focus on business decision-making
Use of data from a wide range of sources
Use of data from structured data sources
